note: Last payment of year 1 at initial 6.00% rate; CFPB CHARM booklet §3.5.1 worked example. $200,000 / 30yr; initial 6% fixed for 1 year.
First payment of year 2 after first reset; index rose 3pp, fully-indexed would be 9% but 2pp periodic cap holds at 8%; engine + CHARM agree to the cent.
Counterfactual: same scenario WITHOUT the 2pp periodic cap; index rises 3pp -> fully-indexed 9%. CHARM cites this as the difference the cap is saving the borrower ($138.70/mo).
